title: Expose ontology-aware runtime input/status CLI for external supervisors
description:
## Context

`imm_supervisor` currently decides Codex/OMX state by scraping tmux pane text and injects prompts with local tmux logic. This creates false busy/idle decisions (for example, stale `Ran` lines below a now-idle `›` prompt) and duplicates ccbot runtime-input behavior.

The desired direction is to route both runtime input and runtime state through ccbot core, using ccbot ontology/control-plane semantics instead of ad-hoc supervisor scraping.

Important boundary:

- `ccbot send` is Telegram delivery surface (`ccbot send --help` now exposes that CLI) and must remain text/file delivery to Telegram.
- Runtime/TUI input needs a separate ontology-aware command/API, not `ccbot send`.

## Proposed work

Add a ccbot core runtime-control CLI/API for external supervisors, e.g. `ccbot inject`, `ccbot input`, or `ccbot runtime-send`, plus a state/status query if one does not already exist.

The command/API should:

- resolve the target through existing ccbot state/ontology where possible (`CCBOT_DIR`, surface/window binding, runtime kind, tmux window id);
- use existing live runtime input-plane checks and fail closed when no writable runtime plane is attached;
- reuse existing `RuntimeInputDriver` / `TmuxManager` paths, including Codex multiline `paste-buffer -p -d` + bare `Enter` semantics;
- provide machine-readable output suitable for supervisors (`--json`), including status such as `input_ready`, `busy`, `blocked_prompt`, `read_only`, `no_live_input_plane`, and target/window metadata;
- keep `ccbot send` documented and tested as Telegram delivery, not runtime injection.

## imm_supervisor consumer need

After this lands, `/home/tools/imm/scripts/imm_tmux_supervisor.sh` should stop scraping pane tails for state and stop embedding tmux prompt delivery. It should ask ccbot for runtime state and call the new ccbot runtime-input command when ccbot says the target is writable/input-ready.

## Acceptance criteria

- New ccbot runtime-input/status command or documented API exists and has `--help`.
- `ccbot send --help` remains Telegram delivery help and tests prove it is not runtime injection.
- Runtime input path uses ccbot ontology/live-input-plane guardrails and fails closed on read-only/no live plane.
- Codex multiline prompts use existing paste-buffer + bare Enter behavior through ccbot, not duplicated supervisor code.
- Tests cover CLI contract and at least one no-live-plane/read-only failure.
- Docs/ontology mention the boundary between delivery surface (`ccbot send`) and runtime input plane.

title: Ignore image ingress in unbound Telegram topics
description: If a Telegram topic/thread is not in the active bound runtime state, image-equivalent ingress should be silent instead of producing bind/unbound guidance or other visible bot reactions. Scope: incoming photo_handler images and sticker_handler image-equivalent stickers/thumbnails. Do not change ccbot send, runtime-input, inject, text, or command bind flows.
acceptance_criteria: Unbound/non-bind photo messages do not download files, do not call session_manager.send_to_window, do not call safe_reply, and do not open/mutate bind flow. Unbound/non-bind sticker messages follow the same silent behavior. Bound photo/sticker delivery behavior remains unchanged. Regression tests cover unbound photo and sticker ingress.
notes:
GitHub issue https://github.com/six-ddc/ccbot/issues/84 was created before the request was clarified to Beads; keep the canonical task here.
Work started 2026-05-02 on p2 /home/tools/ccbot feat/runtime-input-cli. Requirements source of truth is the Bead text; no deploy/restart planned.
Implemented and pushed in 08a7136. Verification: 114 passed in tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py; 23 passed in tests/ccbot/test_docs_contracts.py; targeted ruff clean; full pytest 735 passed; desloppify scan/status/next strict 79.0, queue 0, no open findings for telegram_delivery_policy.py or touched tests. Live deploy not run by request.

title: Generated image success text is not delivered back to Telegram chat
description:
## Correction
This issue is not about delivering the generated image as a Telegram attachment. The expected outcome was a text success/result message in the Telegram chat after the image generation completed.

## Problem
A coding agent generated an improved sticker/image artifact with `gpt-image-2`, and the runtime showed a successful generation result plus a local file path, but the originating Telegram chat did not receive the final text message reporting success/result/path.

Observed example from @ImmArenaBot flow:
- Tool output reported: `Generated Image`
- Saved file: `/home/tools/imm/.codex/generated_images/019de621-9462-7bd0-b2b6-b0b5fe814107/ig_07505510d95b59320169f5ace6313881919be01593ef043a65.png`
- The user expected Telegram text delivery of the success/result message, not an automatic image attachment.
- No such Telegram text result arrived.

## Expected behavior
When the runtime produces a final user-facing success/result message after image generation, compact Telegram delivery should deliver that final text to the bound/originating chat/thread. The text may include the generated file path or a concise result note.

## Actual behavior
The success/result is visible in the runtime transcript, but the Telegram surface does not receive the final text message.

## Boundaries
- Do not require automatic image attachment delivery for this bug.
- Do not repurpose `ccbot send`; it remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- Do not change runtime/TUI input semantics (`runtime-input`/`inject`).
- Preserve topic/thread binding, compact final-message semantics, and CCBOT_DIR resolution guardrails.
- Do not print or expose Telegram tokens/secrets in diagnostics.

## Initial hypothesis
This is likely a compact delivery/final-message detection or routing gap for generated-image success output in the IMM-controlled runtime, not a missing media attachment feature.
acceptance_criteria: After a generated-image operation completes in an IMM/ccbot runtime, the originating Telegram thread receives the final text success/result message; no automatic image attachment is required for this bug; tests/docs cover the compact delivery contract.
notes:
Work started 2026-05-02 on p2 /home/tools/ccbot feat/runtime-input-cli. Requirements source of truth is the Bead text; no deploy/restart planned.
Implemented and pushed in 08a7136. Verification: 114 passed in tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py; 23 passed in tests/ccbot/test_docs_contracts.py; targeted ruff clean; full pytest 735 passed; desloppify scan/status/next strict 79.0, queue 0, no open findings for telegram_delivery_policy.py or touched tests. Live deploy not run by request.
close_reason: Acceptance verified and implementation pushed in 08a7136; generated-image success text now becomes compact terminal Telegram text without automatic media attachment.

title: Run live self-restore smoke for ComfyCodexBot and ImmArenaBot
description: After the durable service/host self-restore code lands, schedule the explicit live-ops gate for both configured bots. Do not run this during unit implementation. Validate safe service/startup restore without blind tmux/service restarts, with special cgroup blast-radius inspection for imm_arena_bot.service.
acceptance_criteria: ComfyCodexBot and ImmArenaBot each have restore inventory evidence, controller env includes CODEX_HOME and OMX_AUTO_UPDATE=0, ccbot runtime-input smoke succeeds, replay ACK appears under the correct CODEX_HOME, and no manual tmux intervention or unrelated tmux session damage occurs.

title: Support artifact-first inbound Telegram audio/video media ingress
description:
Implement artifact-first inbound Telegram audio/video media ingress for IMM/ccbot.

Context:
- OpenAI keys for this account are unavailable/deactivated, so MVP must not require OpenAI transcription.
- Local OSS ASR/diarization artifacts may exist in the IMM environment (`/home/tools/imm/.venv-voice`), but they are future optional enrichment and must not become a ccbot runtime dependency now.
- `ccbot send` remains outbound Telegram delivery; `runtime-input`/`inject` remain runtime/TUI input CLIs.
- Planning artifacts:
  - .omx/plans/prd-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
  - .omx/plans/test-spec-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
  - .omx/plans/ontology-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Bound Telegram audio messages are saved under a safe local media artifact path and forwarded to the runtime with `Audio artifact received.`, `Audio artifact: ...`, metadata, and `Transcript: unavailable (...)` when no valid backend exists.
- Bound Telegram video messages are saved under a safe local media artifact path and forwarded with `Video artifact received.`, `Video artifact: ...`, metadata, and optional thumbnail image marker; if preview is unavailable, runtime text includes `Preview unavailable` and still sends the original artifact path.
- Missing/invalid/deactivated OpenAI credentials do not block artifact delivery and do not trigger OpenAI transcription calls.
- Unbound group-topic audio/video ingress is silent: no download, no safe_reply, no send_to_window, no bind mutation.
- Size/mime/path guardrails run before download where Telegram metadata allows; unsafe/oversize/download failure in a bound topic fails closed with a clear warning.
- Existing voice/photo/document/sticker behavior remains compatible; `ccbot send`, `runtime-input`, and `inject` semantics are unchanged.
- Tests/docs/ontology are updated and targeted pytest + ruff checks pass before deploy.
notes:
Started artifact-first audio/video ingress implementation in p2 /home/tools/ccbot. Deep-interview sanity: no user question needed; ontology checkpoint: audio/video are media artifacts, transcript/preview optional enrichment, ccbot send remains outbound delivery, local OSS ASR remains future work.
Implementation complete on p2 pending deploy. Verification: tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py + test_docs_contracts.py passed (143); full pytest passed (741); ruff check src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; ccbot send --help still exposes outbound --file-path/--file-type. Review: guardrails resolve bound target before download with silent_unbound=True; audio/video do not call OpenAI; video preview is non-fatal; ccbot send/runtime-input/inject semantics unchanged. Desloppify scan ran; queue clear, strict 79.0, unrelated backlog remains.

title: Live-smoke ccbot attachment batching after deploy
description: Follow-up ops gate for the multi-attachment batching change. This implementation was verified by local unit/docs tests only because the coding task explicitly forbade restarting services, launching live runtime panes, or mutating /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot* live state. After an approved deploy window, smoke a Telegram bound topic with: (1) orphan README.md then ~12s later __init__.py + instruction, expecting one runtime input; (2) media_group_id album coalescing; (3) binding-change during debounce fails closed; (4) progress notices show Telegram filenames and no local saved paths.
acceptance_criteria: Approved deploy window used; no manual sessions disturbed; smoke cases pass or failures are captured with logs and rollback/fix issue.

title: Fix OMX stale skill-active/input-lock reconciliation
description: Follow-up from ccbot Codex input ACK investigation. Stale oh-my-codex skill-active-state/input_lock records can deny later planning/interview workflows even when omx state list-active reports no active mode. This is outside the ccbot-only implementation scope; fix in the OMX plugin/source, not plugin cache, with lifecycle reconciliation tests.
acceptance_criteria: Terminal mode state reconciles stale skill-active blockers; completed deep-interview clears/ignores input_lock; legitimate active non-terminal modes still block with actionable state path and cleanup command; omx state list-active either reports or reconciles skill-active blockers.

title: Continue ccbot desloppify cleanup beyond current small batch
description: Follow-up from 2026-05-02 cleanup pass. Current desloppify status after commits: strict 79.0/100, 251 open, focus Cross-module arch 68.0, queue/planned review work remains. Top remaining items include MessageTask variant bag, SessionManager state hub, callback window authorization binding checks, delivery package placement, and audit API width. Keep future batches small, behavior-locked with tests, and avoid mixing production behavior fixes with broad cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Run desloppify next/show review, choose one bounded item, add/keep regression coverage, run targeted tests plus ruff, rescan, and record resolved issues with desloppify plan commit-log.

title: Clear oversized Telegram media download failures
description:
Production regression after artifact-first audio/video ingress.

Evidence:
- User saw Telegram warning: `⚠ Could not download audio artifact for Codex.`
- str `imm_arena_bot.service` log at 2026-05-03 11:13:53 UTC: `Failed to download Telegram audio artifact: File is too big`.
- No audio artifact appeared under `/data/iqdoctor/.ccbot-imm_arena_bot/media`.

Hypothesis/root cause:
ccbot allowed audio up to 50 MiB, but Telegram Bot API remote `getFile`/download is limited to 20 MB. Files between those limits reach `media.get_file()` and raise `File is too big`, which ccbot collapsed into a generic download warning.

Boundaries:
- `ccbot send` remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- `runtime-input`/`inject` remain runtime/TUI input CLIs.
- No OpenAI or local OSS ASR integration in this fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- Known audio/video file_size above the effective Telegram download cap fails before get_file/download and replies with a clear too-large warning.
- Telegram BadRequest/File is too big from get_file or download_to_drive returns the same clear warning instead of generic download failure.
- Files within the effective limit preserve artifact-first forwarding behavior.
- Tests cover audio/video preflight and File is too big regressions plus docs/config contract.
- Fix is committed, pushed, deployed to str, and iqdoctor ccbot services are restarted/verified clean.
notes:
Started regression fix on p2. Root cause confirmed against official Telegram Bot API: getFile/download uses 20 MB remote download limit, below ccbot's current 50 MiB audio guard. Implementing explicit Telegram-download cap and File is too big handling.
Implementation verified locally before deploy. Added CCBOT_MAX_TELEGRAM_DOWNLOAD_BYTES default 20971520, effective min(media cap, Telegram cap), clear preflight and BadRequest/File is too big warnings for audio/video getFile/download. Tests: targeted media regression 6 passed; bot+docs contracts 147 passed; full pytest 745 passed; ruff src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; git diff --check passed. Manual review/anti-slop: ccbot send/runtime-input semantics unchanged; target resolution still happens before download with silent_unbound; no OpenAI/OSS ASR calls added.

title: Run gated live restore for ccbot services
description: Follow-up live operations after code support is merged: back up /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot and /data/iqdoctor/.ccbot-imm_arena_bot, validate exact (user_id,surface_key)+chat_id targets, validate omx --madmax resume syntax, confirm no hermes/ccbot-3/helper collisions, update only target env/state, restart only target services, and smoke Telegram writability with replay evidence ACK.
acceptance_criteria: Both target services are active/enabled; expected tmux windows run OMX/Codex in expected cwd with matching runtime ids; selected surfaces bind to live @window_id; writable smoke has replay evidence ACK; manual sessions untouched.

title: Codify Telegram token env scrubbing in user units
description: During ComfyCodexBot deploy on 2026-05-21, systemd user-manager contained a bogus TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN that overrode CCBOT_DIR/.env because python-dotenv uses override=False. A local drop-in UnsetEnvironment=TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN TELEGRAM_TOKEN restored ccbot.service. Follow up by documenting or templating this guard for all ccbot-style units, including imm_arena_bot if applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Docs or unit templates include UnsetEnvironment for generic TELEGRAM token vars; restart smoke verifies ccbot.service still loads token from CCBOT_DIR/.env; related ops runbook mentions inherited manager env pitfall.

title: Recover Telegram polling when getUpdates stalls
description:
Production bug observed on str @ComfyCodexBot at deployed /tools/ccbot HEAD 92f4e8e.

Symptoms:
- ccbot.service stayed active/running, but stopped consuming Telegram updates after 2026-05-04T18:02:30Z.
- Bot API showed pending_update_count=4; getUpdates had queued text/status/unbind/bind updates for chat -1003685295814 topic 555 from user 3045664.
- Restart drained pending updates.
- Shutdown logged telegram.error.TimedOut: Pool timeout: all connections in getUpdates connection pool occupied.
- Pending /unbind and /bind mutated topic 555 into bind_flow/unbound; operator restored state t:555 -> @0.
- Top-level `/tools/ccbot/.venv/bin/ccbot --help` starts the bot instead of showing CLI help.

Boundaries:
- Develop from p2 /home/tools/ccbot only; no str-local feature edits.
- Preserve `ccbot send` as outbound Telegram delivery and runtime-input/inject as runtime input.
- Do not print tokens/secrets.
acceptance_criteria:
- PTB polling request configuration is explicit and configurable, including getUpdates connection pool size/pool timeout/read/connect/write timeouts.
- A low-risk polling health guard detects pending updates while no update has been handled recently and exits/logs so systemd restarts the bot instead of leaving it wedged active/running.
- `/tools/ccbot/.venv/bin/ccbot --help` and `ccbot -h` print top-level CLI help and do not start the bot or require config secrets.
- `ccbot send --help`, `ccbot runtime-input --help`, and `ccbot inject --help` remain correct.
- Tests/docs cover polling config, watchdog semantics, and top-level help dispatch.
- Fix is committed, pushed, deployed to str, and user ccbot.service is restarted/verified clean.
notes:
Started ralplan/systematic debugging from p2. Evidence checked: str /tools/ccbot HEAD 92f4e8e, ccbot.service active; journal around 2026-05-04 shows last update handling at 18:02:31Z, then restart at 20:42:29Z, shutdown getUpdates cleanup PoolTimeout, and restart drains queued updates. Local PTB 22.7 defaults: get_updates_connection_pool_size=1, get_updates_pool_timeout=1.0, get_updates_read_timeout=5.0.
Implementation complete on p2 before deploy. Added explicit PTB getUpdates pool/timeouts, top-level ccbot --help/-h dispatch before config import, and a Telegram pending-update watchdog that exits for systemd restart when pending updates exist and inbound update handling is stale. Code review/anti-slop: watchdog is liveness-only, does not drain updates or mutate bindings; ccbot send/runtime-input/inject semantics unchanged; TypeHandler marker runs in group -100 before normal handlers. Verification so far: targeted bot/main/docs tests 158 passed; tests/ccbot 749 passed; full pytest 756 passed; ruff src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; git diff --check passed; desloppify scan/status/next ran (strict 79.0, queue cleared, unrelated backlog remains).
Deploy verification complete. Pushed code commit 8a92f6e and reset str /tools/ccbot to origin/feat/runtime-input-cli at 8a92f6e. Restarted iqdoctor user ccbot.service and imm_arena_bot.service. Service evidence: ccbot.service PID 1012205 ActiveEnterTimestamp Tue 2026-05-05 08:54:39 UTC; imm_arena_bot.service PID 1012169 ActiveEnterTimestamp Tue 2026-05-05 08:54:38 UTC. Both journals since restart contain Telegram polling health watchdog started and no PoolTimeout/TimedOut/error matches. Bot API getWebhookInfo through service proxy reports pending_update_count=0 for both bots. Bindings preserved: Comfy t:555 -> @0; IMM t:3 -> @56 and t:142 -> @57; tmux windows/panes live as iqdoctor. Deployed CLI checks: ccbot --help prints top-level help; ccbot send --help includes --file-path/--file-type; ccbot inject --help preserves runtime input alias.
Acceptance delta: user clarified recovery must be automatic, not only diagnostic. Reopened to add deterministic timeout/failure recovery guardrails beyond pending_update_count path: configurable consecutive health-check failure threshold, key=value logs, and tests/docs before redeploy.
Acceptance delta implementation complete before redeploy. Added automatic recovery for repeated timeout-like watchdog health failures while dispatcher progress is stale, with configurable CCBOT_TELEGRAM_POLL_HEALTH_FAILURE_THRESHOLD default 3, key/value event logs, and redacted health error text. Verification: targeted bot/main/docs 161 passed; full pytest 759 passed; ruff src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; git diff --check passed; desloppify scan/status/next ran (strict 79.0, queue cleared; unrelated backlog remains). Manual review: watchdog still does not drain getUpdates or mutate bindings; ccbot send/runtime-input/inject semantics unchanged.
Acceptance delta deploy verification complete. Pushed 38dcb35 and reset str /tools/ccbot to origin/feat/runtime-input-cli at 38dcb35. Deployed import check shows CCBOT_TELEGRAM_POLL_HEALTH_FAILURE_THRESHOLD default 3 and redacted health error text. Restarted iqdoctor user ccbot.service and imm_arena_bot.service. Service evidence after restart: ccbot.service PID 1606610 ActiveEnterTimestamp Tue 2026-05-05 15:53:13 UTC; imm_arena_bot.service PID 1607101 ActiveEnterTimestamp Tue 2026-05-05 15:53:17 UTC. Both journals since restart contain Telegram polling health watchdog started and no PoolTimeout/TimedOut/error matches. Bot API getWebhookInfo through service proxy reports pending_update_count=0 for both bots. Bindings preserved: Comfy t:555 -> @0; IMM t:3 -> @56 and t:142 -> @57; tmux windows live as iqdoctor. Deployed CLI checks: ccbot --help prints top-level help; ccbot send --help includes --file-path/--file-type; ccbot inject --help preserves runtime input alias.
